claims.claim: For a 280Ah LiFePO4 prismatic cell, TR severity intensifies above 50% SOC, with maximum cell temperature increasing sequentially by 35% and 258.3% for 25%, 50%, and 75% SOC tests respectively.
claims.quote: Results demonstrate that TR severity intensifies above 50 % SOC. During the 25 %, 50 %, and 75 % SOC tests, the maximum cell temperature increased sequentially by 35 % and 258.3 %

claims.claim: Gas emissions from a 280Ah LiFePO4 prismatic cell rose by 79.3% and 644.7% sequentially across 25%, 50%, and 75% SOC tests.
claims.quote: while gas emissions rose by 79.3 % and 644.7 %, respectively

claims.claim: The number of gaseous components detected during thermal runaway increased from 15 at 25% SOC to 19 at 50% SOC and 36 at 75% SOC.
claims.quote: Furthermore, 15, 19, and 36 gaseous components are detected under the three conditions

claims.claim: At higher SOCs, CO2 molar fraction in vent gas decreases while H2 molar fraction increases; CO2 decreased from 61% at 25% SOC to 40% at 50% SOC and 12% at 75% SOC, while H2 increased from 7.2% to 17.4% to 26.1%.
claims.quote: At higher SOCs, a notable characteristic of the gas composition is the reduction in CO2 and the elevation in H2. The proportion of CO2 decreases with increasing SOC (from 61 % to 40–12 %), a trend attributed to the alterations in the decomposition pathways of the electrolyte.

claims.claim: The Flammability Hazard Index (FHI) follows the order 50% SOC > 75% SOC > 25% SOC, indicating that flammability risk does not increase monotonically with SOC.
claims.quote: the FHI follows the order 50 % SOC > 75 % SOC > 25 % SOC, indicating that flammability risk does not always increase monotonically with SOC

claims.claim: The lower flammability limit (LFL) for 25% and 50% SOC vent gases ranges from 10% to 15%, while the LFL for 75% SOC vent gas is approximately 5%, indicating higher ignitability at 75% SOC.
claims.quote: The lower flammability limit for 25 % and 50 % SOC (10 %–15 %) is slightly higher than that for 75 % SOC (5 %)

claims.claim: The upper flammability limit (UFL) for 25% and 50% SOC vent gases ranges from 55% to 65%, while the UFL for 75% SOC vent gas falls between 4.7% and 6.6%, with the highest deflagration risk at 50% SOC.
claims.quote: The upper flammability limit for 25 % and 50 % SOC (55 %–65 %) is significantly higher than that for 75 % SOC (4.7 %–6.6 %), with the highest deflagration risk of the gas mixture at 50 % SOC

claims.claim: At 50% SOC, a critical threshold for triggering thermal runaway was identified; cells at 0%, 25%, and 50% SOC did not exhibit significant TR behavior after prolonged heating, reaching Tmax of 77°C, 92.3°C, and 251.1°C respectively.
claims.quote: For all tested cells, 50 % SOC appears to be the critical threshold for triggering TR. After prolonged heating, the cells at 0 %, 25 %, and 50 % SOC exhibited a slight temperature increase followed by a subsequent decrease, with Tmax reaching 77 ℃, 92.3 ℃, and 251.1 ℃

claims.claim: As SOC increases from 0% to 120%, the maximum gas temperature increases from 100.7°C to 418.8°C, and gas production at 120%, 100%, 75%, and 50% SOC is 16.5, 13.9, 7.4, and 2.9 times that at 25% SOC respectively.
claims.quote: During tests at 0–120 % SOC, the maximum gas temperatures reached 100.7 ℃, 120 ℃, 235.5 ℃, 315.9 ℃, 379.9 ℃, and 418.8 ℃, respectively... gas production at 120 %, 100 %, 75 %, and 50 % SOC being 16.5, 13.9, 7.4, and 2.9 times that at 25 % SOC, respectively

claims.claim: The flammable range (RF) for vent gases at 25%, 50%, and 75% SOC are approximately 43.7%, 48.5%, and 17.6% respectively, showing that RF at 75% SOC is not the highest despite greater thermal severity.
claims.quote: The RF corresponding to 25 %, 50 %, and 75 % SOC are approximately 43.7 %, 48.5 %, and 17.6 %, respectively, indicating significant differences in the combustion limits at various SOCs. Contrary to the expectation that higher SOCs always lead to greater combustion risks, the RF of gases produced at 75 % SOC is not the highest.
